mnemonics: Every Loop Uses Euler's Rules: A connected graph is Eulerian if and only if every vertex has an even degree.
keyPoints: A connected graph contains an Eulerian circuit (a cycle that uses every edge exactly once) if and only if every vertex has an even degree.
A connected graph contains an Eulerian path (a path that uses every edge exactly once, but doesn't have to end at the start) if and only if exactly zero or two vertices have an odd degree.
The theorem gives necessary and sufficient conditions, meaning both must be true: if the graph is Eulerian, the condition holds; and if the condition holds, the graph is Eulerian.
commonMistakes: Assuming the theorem applies to disconnected graphs. It specifically states the graph must be connected.
Confusing Eulerian circuits (cycles) with Hamiltonian cycles (which visit every vertex exactly once). Eulerian deals with edges.
Forgetting that 'even degree' includes zero (an isolated vertex has an even degree of 0).
